page: Amazon Web Services g4dn.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores) provided by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. For graphics and compute acceleration, the instance includes a single NVIDIA T4 GPU based on the Turing architecture with 16 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided via a 900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core or accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25%, reflecting the moderate 2.5 GHz clock speed of the Intel Xeon processor. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are robust, with large-block memory read and write speeds ranking in the top 25%. The instance excels in machine learning inference tasks; prompt processing and text generation for small (135M) and medium (7B) parameter models rank in the top 10% tier. Database operations also show strong performance, placing in the top 25% of Passmark benchmarks.

The g4dn.16xlarge offers a balanced hardware profile for workloads requiring a mix of GPU acceleration, high network throughput, and local NVMe storage without the extreme cost of high-end multi-GPU instances. The inclusion of a single NVIDIA T4 GPU makes it highly cost-efficient for small to medium machine learning model inference, video transcoding, and graphics-intensive applications. It is also appropriate for database operations, distributed caching, and application servers that benefit from high network bandwidth and fast local storage, though it is less optimal for single-threaded compute-bound tasks.
description: The Amazon Web Services g4dn.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Built on the AWS Nitro System, it features 64 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, 256 GB of DDR4 memory, and a single NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. Storage is supported by a 900 GB local NVMe SSD, and networking provides a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps. While single-core CPU benchmarks are relatively weak, the instance delivers strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth, database operations, and multi-core tasks. Notably, it achieves top-tier results in LLM inference speed for small and medium models. This hardware profile makes the g4dn.16xlarge highly cost-efficient for machine learning inference, video transcoding, and database workloads requiring moderate GPU acceleration and high network throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, an NVIDIA T4 GPU, and a 900 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for machine learning inference and graphics workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, an NVIDIA T4 GPU, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring an NVIDIA T4 GPU, local NVMe storage, and high network bandwidth for machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 256 GB DDR4 memory
Equipped with a single NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB VRAM
Includes a 900 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
Top-tier performance for small and medium LLM inference
Best suited for machine learning inference and database operations
